vocab.example: I saved this email as a draft, I'll send it later.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi đã lưu email này thành bản nháp, tôi sẽ gửi sau.

vocab.example: Please add your phone number to your email signature.
vocab.example_vi: Vui lòng thêm số điện thoại vào chữ ký email của bạn.

vocab.example: Try to keep your emails concise and clear.
vocab.example_vi: Hãy cố gắng viết email ngắn gọn và rõ ràng.

vocab.example: Be careful with your tone when writing to a client.
vocab.example_vi: Hãy cẩn thận với giọng điệu khi viết cho khách hàng.

vocab.example: Can you check the whole email thread before replying?
vocab.example_vi: Bạn kiểm tra toàn bộ chuỗi email trước khi trả lời được không?

vocab.example: There was a misunderstanding about the meeting time.
vocab.example_vi: Có một sự hiểu lầm về giờ họp.

vocab.example: Always be polite, even when the client is upset.
vocab.example_vi: Luôn lịch sự, ngay cả khi khách hàng đang khó chịu.

vocab.example: Please reply as soon as possible — it's urgent.
vocab.example_vi: Vui lòng trả lời càng sớm càng tốt — việc này khẩn cấp.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Linh, there was a misunderstanding about the meeting time.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Really? Let me check the whole email thread.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: The client thought the meeting was at three, but we meant three in their time zone.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: I see. Let's write a concise email to clarify this quickly.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Good idea. We should keep a polite tone, even though it's a bit awkward.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Agreed. I'll write a draft now and send it to you first.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Thanks. Please reply as soon as possible — the meeting is in an hour.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: On it. I'll also add my signature with my phone number, just in case.

questions.q: Why does Minh want a fast reply?
options: The meeting is in an hour

shadowing: There was a misunderstanding about the meeting time with the client.
Let's write a concise email to clarify this quickly before it's too late.
We should keep a polite tone, even though it's a bit awkward.
I'll write a draft now and send it to you first for review.
Please reply as soon as possible, because the meeting is in an hour.

interview.question: How do you make sure your written communication, like emails, is clear and professional?
tips_vi: Nêu nguyên tắc cụ thể: ngắn gọn, đọc lại trước khi gửi, chú ý giọng điệu
Có thể nhắc đến việc tránh viết khi đang tức giận
Kết nối với ví dụ thực tế nếu có
interview.sample_answer: I always try to keep my emails concise, with a clear subject line and short paragraphs, so the reader can understand quickly. Before sending, I read the email one more time to check the tone, especially when writing to a client or someone I don't know well. If the topic is sensitive, I try to stay polite and neutral, even if I'm frustrated inside. I also avoid writing long emails late at night, because I might sound careless or too direct without meaning to. When something is really important, I add a clear subject line so the reader knows it needs quick attention. This habit has helped me avoid several misunderstandings in my past jobs, and my manager once told me my emails are easy to follow, which I was proud of.

interview.question: Describe a time a misunderstanding happened over email. How did you resolve it?
tips_vi: Dùng STAR: nêu nguyên nhân hiểu lầm cụ thể
Nhấn mạnh cách bạn làm rõ mà không đổ lỗi cho ai
Kết thúc bằng kết quả và bài học
interview.sample_answer: Once, a client misunderstood a meeting time because we didn't mention the time zone clearly in our first email. As soon as I noticed the confusion in the email thread, I wrote a short, polite email to clarify the correct time immediately, instead of waiting for someone else to fix it. I also apologized for the confusion, even though it was a simple mistake and nobody was really at fault. The client appreciated the quick response, and the meeting still happened on time without a big problem. Since then, I always include the time zone clearly in any scheduling email, and I even add it to our team's email template so this mistake doesn't happen again.
